deck: How an idea born in academic research at UCSD became SureAdhere, a commercial venture using video directly observed therapy to transform tuberculosis treatment, told alongside one patient's year on the platform.
seo_description: The SureAdhere story: turning UCSD research into a commercial venture using video directly observed therapy to transform tuberculosis treatment.
og_description: The SureAdhere story: academic research to commercial scale, using video DOT to transform tuberculosis treatment.
about: Dimagi's Jonathan Jackson and Kelly Collins, director of digital adherence, sit down with Chris, a tuberculosis survivor and advocate who was infected through a contaminated cadaver bone during back surgery during the height of Covid. Chris walks through his diagnosis, his year long treatment course, and how video directly observed therapy, or VDOT, let him keep his daily life while staying on his medication.

The conversation grounds the SureAdhere story in a real patient experience. Chris describes downloading the app, recording his doses from a coffee plantation in the Colombian Andes, and the trust he placed in Denver Department of Public Health. He also offers candid product feedback on privacy, recording prompts, and the need for in-platform chat, and explains why he became an advocate with We Are TB to push for more research, funding, and patient voice.

pull_quote.text: It gave me my schedule back. It gave me some ownership of my day. As long as I had wifi connection, it was great. You know, towards the end of my 365 days, I was traveling in the Colombian Andes, and here I am using SureAdhere to VDOT my drug intake.

bullets: From research to commercial scale: turning an idea licensed from UCSD into SureAdhere, a company signing up health departments
What VDOT actually is: video directly observed therapy and how it transforms a year long TB treatment course
A patient's view: Chris on contracting TB from a contaminated cadaver bone and navigating treatment during Covid
Freedom and ownership: recording doses while traveling, from Denver to a coffee plantation in the Colombian Andes
Privacy and trust: meeting HIPAA and GDPR standards, and why patient education still matters when some patients hesitate
Product feedback in real time: recording prompts, an in-platform chat feature, and the move toward wraparound virtual care
Becoming an advocate: Chris on his work with We Are TB to elevate patient voices and push for more research and funding
